channel.title: Openclaw on Remote Web Admin | Managed OpenClaw AI Assistant Service
channel.description: Recent content in Openclaw on Remote Web Admin | Managed OpenClaw AI Assistant Service

item.title: How to Install OpenClaw on a $5 VPS (DigitalOcean & Hetzner Guide)
item.description:
How to Install OpenClaw on a $5 VPS (DigitalOcean & Hetzner Guide)

Running your own OpenClaw AI assistant starts with a single virtual private server. For most personal users, a $5–6/month VPS from DigitalOcean or Hetzner is more than enough to host a responsive, always-on AI gateway that connects to WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, and iMessage. This guide walks you through every step - from provisioning the server to your first working conversation.

item.title: How to Connect WhatsApp to OpenClaw - Step-by-Step 2026 Guide
item.description:
How to Connect WhatsApp to OpenClaw - Step-by-Step 2026 Guide

Getting WhatsApp connected to OpenClaw is the most powerful - and the most involved - channel integration you can make. Unlike Telegram, which requires just a bot token, WhatsApp uses Meta’s Business API and requires several layers of verification. This guide explains every step, including the parts that frequently trip people up.

Why WhatsApp Is Worth the Effort

WhatsApp has over 2 billion active users. For many people and businesses, it’s the primary communication channel. Connecting it to your self-hosted OpenClaw AI assistant means you can have intelligent, context-aware AI responses arrive directly in WhatsApp conversations - without routing your messages through any third-party AI platform.

item.title: How to Connect Telegram to Your OpenClaw AI Assistant
item.description:
How to Connect Telegram to Your OpenClaw AI Assistant

If you are new to OpenClaw channel integrations, Telegram is the best place to start. Unlike WhatsApp, which requires business verification and Meta Developer Console setup, Telegram takes about five minutes and requires nothing more than a Telegram account. By the end of this guide, you will have a fully working Telegram AI assistant powered by OpenClaw.

Why Telegram First?

Telegram’s bot platform is open, well-documented, and imposes no business verification requirements. You create a bot with a single conversation in the BotFather interface, receive an API token, and paste it into your OpenClaw config. There’s no webhook verification dance, no approval process, and no phone number restrictions. It’s the fastest way to see OpenClaw in action.

item.title: OpenClaw on Discord: Complete Setup Guide for Teams
item.description:
OpenClaw on Discord: Complete Setup Guide for Teams

Discord has become a primary workspace for remote teams, developer communities, and collaborative projects. Connecting OpenClaw to Discord gives every member of your server access to a self-hosted AI assistant - one that lives in your own infrastructure and doesn’t route your conversations through third-party AI platforms. This guide covers the entire setup, from creating the Discord application to your first AI response in a channel.

item.title: How to Add iMessage to OpenClaw on macOS
item.description:
How to Add iMessage to OpenClaw on macOS

The iMessage integration for OpenClaw is the most technically demanding of the four supported channels - but it unlocks something no other channel can offer: a fully private, end-to-end encrypted AI assistant accessible via Apple’s native messaging platform. If you use iMessage as your primary messaging app on iPhone and Mac, this guide explains exactly what’s involved and how to set it up.

item.title: Securing Your OpenClaw Instance: SSL, Firewall & Auth Best Practices
item.description:
Securing Your OpenClaw Instance: SSL, Firewall & Auth Best Practices

A self-hosted OpenClaw AI assistant gives you full control over your data and conversations - but that control comes with responsibility. An improperly secured server exposes your AI provider API keys, your conversation history, and potentially your entire VPS to the internet. This guide walks through the essential hardening steps every OpenClaw installation should have in place.

1. Nginx SSL Termination with Let’s Encrypt

If you followed the VPS install guide, you already have SSL configured. Here’s how to verify it’s set up correctly and auto-renewing.

item.title: OpenClaw Plugins: How to Extend Your AI Assistant's Capabilities
item.description:
OpenClaw Plugins: How to Extend Your AI Assistant’s Capabilities

Out of the box, OpenClaw connects your messaging channels to an AI provider and handles conversation routing. But the real power of the platform comes from its plugin architecture - a modular system that lets you add tools, connect external services, and build specialised agent behaviours on top of the core gateway. This guide explains how the plugin system works and how to add the most useful extensions.

item.title: OpenClaw vs ChatGPT: Which Personal AI Assistant Is Right for You?
item.description:
OpenClaw vs ChatGPT: Which Personal AI Assistant Is Right for You?

The personal AI assistant market has two broad categories: hosted platforms you sign up for, and self-hosted open-source gateways you control. ChatGPT is the most recognisable name in the first category. OpenClaw is rapidly becoming the leading option in the second. This comparison breaks down the key differences to help you decide which is right for your situation.

Head-to-Head Comparison

Factor OpenClaw ChatGPT
Privacy Data stays on your server Conversations may train OpenAI models
Monthly cost ~$6–11/mo (VPS + API) $20/mo (Plus) or $200/mo (Pro)
Customisation Full control - system prompts, plugins, personas Limited (custom instructions only)
Messaging channels WhatsApp, Telegram, Discord, iMessage Web browser + iOS/Android app only
AI model choice OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or local Ollama GPT-4o, o1, o3 (OpenAI only)
Setup time 30–60 minutes (or handled by RemoteWebAdmin) Instant (sign up and go)
Always-on availability Requires a running VPS or Mac Always available via OpenAI infrastructure

Privacy: Where OpenClaw Has a Clear Advantage

When you use ChatGPT, your conversations are processed on OpenAI’s servers. OpenAI’s data usage policy allows them to use your conversations to improve their models (unless you opt out via account settings, which many users never find or do). Even with opt-out, your data still transits OpenAI’s infrastructure.

item.title: Self-Hosted AI Assistant: Why Privacy-First Is the Future
item.description:
Self-Hosted AI Assistant: Why Privacy-First Is the Future

Most people accept a hidden cost when they use free or low-cost AI tools: their data. Conversations with hosted AI assistants are routinely used to improve models, retained for legal compliance periods, and processed through multiple tiers of cloud infrastructure owned by third parties. For many users, this is an acceptable trade-off. For a growing number - particularly professionals with confidential work, individuals in privacy-sensitive roles, and businesses operating under regulatory frameworks - it is not. Self-hosted AI assistants like OpenClaw offer an alternative.

item.title: WhatsApp AI Assistant: The Complete 2026 Guide
item.description:
WhatsApp AI Assistant: The Complete 2026 Guide

WhatsApp is the world’s most-used messaging platform, with over 2 billion active users. Adding an AI assistant to your WhatsApp conversations - one that can answer questions, draft messages, summarise documents, and help you think through problems - is one of the highest-leverage things you can do with modern AI. But the path to a WhatsApp AI assistant is not as straightforward as it might seem. This guide covers all the options.

item.title: The Real Cost of Running Your Own AI Assistant (And How to Keep It Low)
item.description:
The Real Cost of Running Your Own AI Assistant (And How to Keep It Low)

One of the most common questions about self-hosted AI assistants is: “OK, but how much does it actually cost?” The honest answer is: less than you probably think - and significantly less than hosted AI subscriptions for anyone who uses AI regularly. Here’s a transparent breakdown of every cost involved.

The Cost Components

Running an OpenClaw AI assistant on your own infrastructure involves two recurring costs and one optional one-time cost.

item.title: Personal AI Assistant for Business: ROI and Use Cases in 2026
item.description:
Personal AI Assistant for Business: ROI and Use Cases in 2026

AI assistants are no longer a curiosity for early adopters. In 2026, they are a productivity tool with measurable return on investment - one that most knowledge workers are only beginning to fully exploit. This article examines the concrete business use cases where a personal AI assistant delivers the most value, how to quantify that value, and why OpenClaw Business deployment is the right infrastructure choice for teams.
